assertions.assertion: Defines a BasicWorkflow without an explicit simulator
assertions.evidence: Line 42: workflow = bf.BasicWorkflow(adapter=adapter, inference_network=inference_net) — no simulator argument passed.

assertions.assertion: Uses the clean images as inference_variables and the noisy images as inference_conditions
assertions.evidence: Lines 75-78: adapter routes clean images as inference_variables and blurry images as inference_conditions via .concatenate() calls.

assertions.assertion: Does NOT flatten any of the images
assertions.evidence: Images retained as (H, W, C) tensors throughout. No .reshape(-1) or .flatten() call anywhere in the code.

assertions.assertion: Does NOT use a summary network (SetTransformer, DeepSet, ConvolutionalNetwork, etc.)
assertions.evidence: BasicWorkflow instantiated without summary_network argument. No summary_net defined anywhere in the script.

assertions.assertion: Uses a diffusion model or flow matching with a UNet architecture as a subnet
assertions.evidence: Lines 33-38: inference_net = bf.networks.DiffusionModel(subnet=bf.networks.UNet(...)). UNet used as the denoising backbone.

assertions.assertion: Uses the fit_offline method of a BasicWorkflow
assertions.evidence: Line 88: history = workflow.fit_offline(train_data=train_data, ...) — fit_offline called on the BasicWorkflow.

assertions.assertion: Uses bf.Adapter() with an explicit adapter chain (not just naming kwargs)
assertions.evidence: Lines 55-78: bf.Adapter() with chained .convert_dtype(), .rename(), .concatenate() calls — explicit method chain.

assertions.assertion: Ensures conditioning tensor is spatially concatenable with the image target — same (B, H, W, C/D) shape
assertions.evidence: Lines 20-25: Gaussian blur applied to produce blurry images of identical (28, 28, 1) shape as clean targets. Adapter routes both without reshaping.

assertions.assertion: Passes validation_data= to the fit_offline call
Saves history.history as JSON and runs inspect_training or equivalent convergence check
assertions.evidence: Lines 95-97: json.dump(history.history, f) saves to history.json. Line 100: inspect_history(history.history) called and convergence report printed.

assertions.assertion: Does NOT run scripts/check_diagnostics.py or conventional in-silico diagnostics for low-dimensional problems
assertions.evidence: No call to check_diagnostics or compute_default_diagnostics anywhere. Analysis notes explicitly state: 'ECE/NRMSE diagnostics do not apply to image targets.'

assertions.assertion: Creates and inspects a few posterior clean images for a few test examples (visual sample grid)
assertions.evidence: Lines 115-130: generates 16 posterior samples per test image and plots a grid of noisy input, posterior samples, and clean ground truth.

Analysis notes line 5: 'Following references/image-generation.md: use DiffusionModel + UNet, no summary network, images as inference_conditions'. Code structure matches image-generation.md recommendations.
